question_text: What does HTML stand for?
explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language — the standard language to build web pages.

question_text: Which tag is considered the root of every HTML document?
explanation: The <html> tag wraps the entire document content and is the top-level root element.

question_text: In which section do we usually place the page title?
explanation: The <title> tag belongs inside <head> and sets the browser tab / bookmark title.

question_text: Which tag contains all the content that users actually see on the page?
explanation: The <body> tag holds visible content such as text

question_text: What is the recommended DOCTYPE declaration for modern HTML documents?
explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> is the short and correct declaration that triggers standards mode in browsers.

question_text: Which heading level is the largest and carries the highest importance?
explanation: <h1> is the top-level heading — usually used for the main page title.

question_text: Which heading tag produces the smallest text size among the standard headings?
explanation: <h6> is the lowest importance and smallest heading level.

question_text: What is the standard tag used to define a paragraph of text?
explanation:
The

tag defines a paragraph (block-level element with default margins).

question_text: Which empty tag is used to insert a single line break?
explanation: forces a line break without starting a new paragraph.

question_text: Which tag creates a thematic horizontal line (usually for separating content)?
explanation: <hr> represents a thematic break between paragraph-level elements.

question_text: What is the correct self-closing tag to embed an image in HTML?
explanation: is self-closing and used to embed images (src attribute required).

question_text: Which attribute specifies the URL or file path of an image in the tag?
explanation: src stands for source and points to the image location.

question_text: What does the alt attribute provide for an tag?
explanation: alt text is shown if the image fails to load and is used by screen readers.

question_text: Which tag is a generic block-level container commonly used for layout before semantic elements became popular?
explanation: <div> has no semantic meaning — it is used purely for grouping and styling.

question_text: Which tag represents introductory content or a group of introductory elements (logo
explanation: <header>
mdn_link: <header> is used for introductory or navigational content at the top of a section or page.

question_text: What tag should be used for the main content area of a page (excluding headers
explanation: <main>
mdn_link: <main> identifies the dominant content of the document.

question_text: Which tag is appropriate for content that stands alone (blog post
explanation: <article>

question_text: Which tag is typically used for sidebar content or call-out boxes?
explanation: <aside> holds content tangentially related to the main content (sidebars

question_text: Which tag is self-contained and often used with <figcaption> for images
explanation: C
topic: <figure> groups media with an optional caption.

question_text: What is the purpose of the <small> tag in modern HTML?
explanation: <small> is used for disclaimers

question_text: Which tag defines a caption or legend for a <figure> element?
explanation: <figcaption> provides a caption for the parent <figure>.

question_text: Which attribute controls the order in which elements receive focus when using the Tab key?
explanation: tabindex defines the tab navigation order (0 = natural order

question_text: Which tag is used to define emphasized (italic-style) text with semantic importance?
explanation: <em> indicates stress emphasis (usually rendered italic).

question_text: Which tag indicates strong importance (usually bold)?
explanation: <strong> conveys strong importance or seriousness.

question_text: What is the correct tag for an inline quotation with citation?
explanation: <q> is for short inline quotations (adds automatic quotes in most browsers).

question_text: Which tag is used for longer quotations that usually start on a new line?
explanation: <blockquote> is for long block-level quotations (usually indented).

question_text: Which attribute is commonly used with <blockquote> to indicate the source URL?
explanation: cite attribute on <blockquote> points to the source (not visible

question_text: Which tag defines the caption or title for a table?
explanation: <caption> provides a title or summary for the table (should be the first child of <table>).

question_text: What is the semantic tag for the footer of a page or section?
explanation: <footer> contains footer information: copyright

question_text: Which tag groups table header cells?
explanation: <thead> defines the header section of a table (usually contains <tr> with <th> cells).

question_text: What tag is used for table footer content (summary rows
explanation: A
topic: <tfoot> groups footer rows (often placed before <tbody> in source for rendering reasons).

question_text: Which attribute makes a table cell span multiple columns?
explanation: colspan defines how many columns a <td> or <th> should cover.

question_text: Which attribute makes a table cell span multiple rows?
explanation: rowspan controls how many rows a cell occupies.

question_text: What is the semantic tag for navigation links?
explanation: <nav> is used for major navigation blocks (menu

question_text: Which tag represents a self-contained piece of content that can be independently distributed?
explanation: <article> is ideal for blog posts

question_text: What does HTML stand for?
explanation: HTML = HyperText Markup Language — foundation of web page structure.

question_text: Why do modern browsers require the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration?
explanation: Without DOCTYPE (or wrong one) browser may enter quirks mode → inconsistent rendering.

question_text: When should you place meta charset="UTF-8" in an HTML file?
option_c: As the last tag in document
explanation: It must appear early (ideally first in <head>) to correctly interpret special characters before page renders.

question_text: How many root elements can an HTML document have?
explanation: There must be exactly one <html> root element — everything else is inside it.

question_text: Why is it recommended to keep <script> tags at the end of <body> (before </body>)?
option_b: To make page load faster visually
explanation: Placing scripts at the end prevents render-blocking — page content appears sooner.

question_text: Which heading tag should usually be used only once per page for accessibility?
explanation: <h1> should represent the main topic — multiple <h1>s can confuse screen readers and SEO.

question_text: When would you choose <em> over <i> in modern HTML?
option_a: When you want italic styling without meaning
option_c: When text needs semantic stress emphasis
explanation: <em> adds semantic importance/stress (usually italic)

question_text: Why do screen readers announce <strong> text differently from normal text?
explanation: <strong> conveys strong importance — screen readers may change tone/volume to emphasize it.

question_text: How can you make an image decorative (not announced by screen readers)?
explanation: alt="" tells screen readers to skip the image (purely decorative).

question_text: When should you prefer
option_a: When image is part of main content
option_b: When image needs caption and is self-contained
explanation: <figure> + <figcaption> is semantic for self-contained illustrated content with caption.

question_text: Why is it better to use <nav> for main navigation links instead of plain <div>?
option_c: It improves SEO and accessibility semantics
explanation: <nav> clearly communicates that the content is navigation — helps screen readers and search engines.

question_text: How should you make an image decorative so screen readers ignore it?
explanation: alt="" tells assistive tech the image is purely decorative and can be skipped.

question_text: When is it better to use
option_c: When image needs semantic grouping and caption
explanation: <figure> gives semantic meaning to self-contained illustrated content with caption.

question_text: Why do we prefer semantic tags like <nav>
explanation: They are required for HTML5 validation
youtube_english: search engines and future tools understand page structure.

question_text: What does the <main> tag represent?
option_d: Primary unique content of the document
explanation: <main> identifies the dominant
topic: unique content (should appear only once per page).

question_text: Which tag should contain copyright info
option_a: author credits or related links at the bottom?
explanation: D
topic: <footer> is the semantic place for closing information (per section or whole page).

question_text: How do you make a link open in a new browser tab?
explanation: target="_blank" opens link in new tab (also consider rel="noopener" for security).

question_text: Why should you avoid using target="_blank" without rel="noopener noreferrer"?
explanation: Without rel="noopener"
topic: new tab can access opener window → potential phishing/tabnabbing attack.

question_text: Which attribute provides additional information about a link (shown as tooltip on hover)?
explanation: title attribute displays tooltip text on mouse hover.

question_text: When should you use <ol> instead of <ul>?
explanation: <ol> (ordered list) is correct for sequences

question_text: What is the semantic difference between <strong> and <b>?
explanation: <b> is bold
youtube_kannada: <b> is purely visual bold (no meaning added).

question_text: Why is <em> preferred over <i> when text needs emphasis?
explanation: <em> indicates stress/emphasis — screen readers may change tone; <i> is stylistic only.

question_text: Which tag should be used for a short inline quote with automatic quotation marks?
explanation: <q> is for short quotes — browsers usually add “ ” automatically.

question_text: When should you use <blockquote> instead of <q>?
explanation: <blockquote> is block-level and usually indented — correct for longer quotations.

question_text: How do you add a citation/source URL to a <blockquote> semantically?
option_d: Use cite attribute on the <blockquote> tag
explanation: cite="https://…" on <blockquote> provides machine-readable source (not visible).

question_text: Which tag defines a caption for a table?
explanation: <caption> provides title/summary for table — should be first child of <table>.

question_text: Why is <thead> useful in a table?
explanation: <thead> semantically identifies header rows — helps screen readers and styling.

question_text: How can a single table cell span across 3 columns?
explanation: colspan="3" makes cell cover 3 columns.

question_text: Which attribute makes a table cell span multiple rows?
explanation: rowspan="n" lets a <td>/<th> cover n rows.

question_text: Why is it recommended to use scope="col" or scope="row" on <th> cells?
option_c: To help screen readers understand table structure
explanation: scope attribute tells screen readers whether header applies to column or row.

question_text: When should you add role="presentation" to a <table>?
option_a: When table is used for layout (old practice)
explanation: role="presentation" removes table semantics for pure-layout tables (avoid if possible — use CSS grid/flex instead).
